notes: foreground-subagent for mistral-vibe: the parent uses the builtin Task tool to spawn ONE synchronous explore subagent that reads a seeded README.md and reports its heading, then the parent replies 'done'. Spec asserts the arc: parent ready→working at spawn, a child session materializes under <parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl (task.py save_dir=session_dir/'agents'; session_logger.py persists messages.jsonl+meta.json), the child is parent_linked to the parent, the parent stays working across the child's lifetime (the Task tool-call awaits the child), and parent working→ready once the child ends. The parent↔child link is now LIVE (commit 3d89d5e9): deriveVibeParentSessionID reads the parent off the .../<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl path — matching the id sessionIDFromPath registers the parent under — and the existing subagent machinery emits parent_linked, holds the parent working, and tracks SubagentCount. record ports the driver's wait_turn seam (driver=gap:wait_turn) before driving. Single-birth adapter (session id = the transcript directory, sessionIDFromPath), birth anchored to start unpinned. Observations: model mistral-medium-3.5 (live config.active_model), tokens_nonzero (sidecar stats.context_tokens → turn_done), cost_nonzero=false (parser never reads stats.session_cost; mistral-vibe absent from core/pkg/capacity). BACKFLOW (re-spec): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter -- birth re-modeled as presession_birth -> session_birth (new_session, different session_id) templated from the codex sibling; observations now assert agent only (cost & tokens are sidecar-only, unverifiable in the offline replay golden, like kiro-cli), superseding any model/tokens claim above. RECORD BACKFLOW: vibe flushes the parent turn AND the full child subagent lifecycle atomically at turn-end, so the child transcript_new is discovered near-simultaneously with (in this recording, 22ms BEFORE) parent_turn_start — the causal ordering is not observable. child_session_birth is therefore anchored to presession_birth (the post-launch flush window), NOT parent_turn_start; parent_linked, intermediate_working (parent working across the child lifetime) and parent_turn_end still bind the pair. The load-bearing observable — parent_linked (the 3d89d5e9 child->parent fix) — fires correctly.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the parent transcript dir exists)
Parent session_<...> reconciles (different session_id) when its transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the parent session (matched by cwd)
User Task-tool prompt drives the parent ready -> working (spec bullet 1)

Subagent transcript materializes under <parent>/agents/<explore_...>/messages.jsonl -- fswatcher discovers a new SessionState for the child [anchored to presession_birth: vibe atomic per-turn flush makes child vs parent discovery order nondeterministic]

Detector emits parent_linked binding child.session_id -> parent.session_id (spec bullet 2: ParentSessionID = parent.session_id). deriveVibeParentSessionID resolves the parent off the child's .../agents/<child>/ path

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the child is alive -- the Task tool-call awaits the child loop (spec bullet 4)
After the child reports back and the parent replies 'done' (turn_done), the parent goes working -> ready (spec bullet 5)
